original: American amateur astronomerHenry Drapertakes the 1st ever photograph of the Orion Nebula
1st Boeing 747 jumbo jet rolls out
"The Flintstones," the first animated sitcom created byHanna-Barbera, premieres on ABC in the US
First manned rocket plane flight, by automaker Fritz von Opel
Hans Geiger, German physicist (co-inventor of the Geiger counter), born in Neustadt an der Weinstraße, German Empire (d. 1945)
1st nuclear submarine, USS Nautilus, commissioned by the US Navy
Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthsmacks his MLB record 60th home run off Tom Zachary in 8th inning of New York's 4-2 win over Washington Senators at Yankee Stadium
MLB Oakland A's outfielderRickey Hendersonsets AL stolen base record with his 97th in A's 5-1 win over Chicago White Sox; breaks Ty Cobb's mark of 96 set in 1915
USSR's Kosmos 186 & 188 complete 1st automatic docking
Ethernet specifications are published by Xerox in collaboration withInteland Digital Equipment Corporation
Irving B. Kahn, American inventor of the teleprompter, born in Newark, New Jersey (d. 1994)
Auguste/Jacques Piccard dives with bathosphere to 3150 m (record)
The Boulder Dam (later theHooverDam), astride the border of Arizona and Nevada, is dedicated by US PresidentFranklin D. Roosevelt
Herbert Maxwell Strong, American physicist and inventor (1st to synthesize diamonds as part of Project Superpressure), born in Wooster, Ohio (d. 2002)[1]
Barry Marshall, Australian physician, (1995 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for discovery bacterium Helicobacter pylori causes peptic ulcers), born in Kalgoorlie, Australia
Mexican-American labor leader César Chávez founds the United Farm Workers
AdmiralChester Nimitz's B-17 finds Guadalcanal using a National Geographic map
Robinson Crusoe is shipwrecked (according toDaniel Defoe's famous novel)
On Howdy Doody's last show Clarabelle finally talks "Goodbye Kids"
California Angel Mike Witt, pitches a perfect game over Texas Rangers, 1-0
Detroit's future Baseball Hall of Fame first basemanHank Greenbergsmacks an AL pennant-winning grand slam on the final day of the season as the Tigers beat St. Louis Browns, 6-3
Anesthetic ether is used for the first time by American dentist Dr. William Morton, who extracts a tooth
13 Black soldiers among the first Black soldiers to be honored with US Medal of Honor for leading charges against Confederate fortifications during Battle of New Market Heights, Virginia
Columbia Rediviva leaves Boston on the first US voyage to circumnavigate the globe
Samuel Slocum patents the stapler
Last Washington Senator home game, Yanks win career 5th forfeit game Yanks trailing 4-2 in 9th with 2 outs, fans rush field
Louise Ritter, US, jumps 6'8" to win Olympic gold medal
Space shuttle STS-68 (Endeavour 7), launches into orbit
TheDalai Lamaunveils the Canadian Tribute to Human Rights monument in Canada's capital city of Ottawa, Ontario
West German Foreign Minister Hans-Dietrich Genscher grants escaping East Germans free passage to the West in speech from West German embassy balcony in Prague
Soviet Union beats Yugoslavia 76-63 to win the men's basketball gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; US finishes 3rd; final time US field non-NBA team
Great Flood hits New Zealand's South Island
Apollo program's ALSEP experiment packages left on the Moon are shut down due to US budget cuts
1st congress of International Astronautical Federation opens in Paris
Xiomara Castro, Honduran politician (1st female President of Honduras 2022-), born in Santa Bárbara, Honduras
Frankie Lymon, American pop singer (The Teenagers - "Why Do Fools Fall in Love"), born in New York City (d. 1968)
Frank Rijkaard, Dutch soccer midfielder (73 caps; Ajax, AC Milan) and manager (Netherlands, Saudi Araboa, FC Barcelona), born in Amsterdam, Netherlands
Barry Williams, American actor (Greg-Brady Bunch), born in Santa Monica, California
Harry Winston Jerome, Canadian 100m runner (Olympic bronze 1964), born in Sask, Canada
Marilyn McCoo, American soul singer (the 5th Dimension; "You Don't Have To Be A Star") and TV host (Solid Gold), born in Jersey City, New Jersey
Héctor Lavoe [Pérez], Puerto Rican salsa singer, born in Machuelo Abajo, Ponce, Puerto Rico (d. 1993)
Patrice Rushen, American jazz and R&B pianist and singer and songwriter ("Forget Me Nots"), born in Los Angeles, California
Marc Bolan, British rock vocalist and guitarist (T-Rex - "Bang A Gong (Get It On)"; "Jeepster"), born in London (d. 1977)
Johnny Mathis, American pop vocalist ("Chances Are"; "Wild Is The Wind"; The Twelfth of Never"), born in Gilmer, Texas
George Gershwin's folk opera "Porgy and Bess" world premiere at the Colonial Theatre in Boston, Massachusetts
Mozart's opera "The Magic Flute," with a German libretto by Emanuel Schikaneder, premieres at Schikaneder's Freihaus-Theater auf der Wieden in Vienna, Austria

Roy Orbison and friends (including James Burton; Bonnie Raitt,Bruce Springsteen, Elvis Costello, Tom Waits, and k.d. lang) film performance at the Ambassador Hotel's Coconut Grove nightclub in Los Angeles, California for Cinemax cable TV special "A Black and White Night"
BBC starts its own popular music radio station (Radio 1)
Mel Gray begins NFL streak of 121 consecutive game receptions
Bechuanaland, in southern Africa, gains independence from Great Britain, becomes Republic of Botswana
Britain first evacuates citizens in anticipation of war.
In the first televised college football game, Fordham beats Waynesburg, 34-7 at Randalls Island, NY
Peter Stuyvesant forbids tennis playing during religious services in New Netherland, the first mention of tennis in the US
Berlin/Hart/Heyman/Myers ballet "As Thousands Cheer," premieres in NYC
French Guinee becomes independent republic Guinea
West Germany signs trade agreement with East Germany
1st Portuguese immigrants arrive in Hawaii
Palace of Fine Arts reopens (1st time during 1915 exposition)
Jalal al-Din Muhammad Rumi, Persian mystic and poet, born in Khorassan, Afghanistan (d. 1273)
Jalaluddin Muhammad Rumi, Persian poet, scholar and mystic, born in Balkh, [modern Afghanistan] (d. 1273)[1]
